candidates.stmt:
I have been selected to stand for Immanuel Ward in Oswaldtwistle in this May's Hyndburn Borough Council elections.

But who I am as a person is quite simple.

I’m a husband and a father of 3, born and raised in Oswaldtwistle, it’s where I live and where I’m bringing up my family.

In the May local elections, you will decide who you think will be the best councillor for our town. That’s why I am putting myself forward again to represent my hometown.

In my 5 years as a councillor for Oswaldtwistle, I have always put residents first;

•Played an active role in the fight against the cemetary proposal.

•Worked closely with residents to oppose planning applications in our town.

•Worked tirelessly with the former owners to secure an investment into the derelict Rhyddings Mill site now a Home Bargains store and a new housing estate on Stonebridge lane.

•Help keep Oswaldtwistle clean and tidy by regularly litter picking with my son Oliver.

•Led the campaign to get Article 4 direction for HMOs implemented across the whole of Hyndburn.

•Fighting for democracy for residents when Labour and others wanted to cancel the elections.

and much more !!!!

Everybody knows how hard I work for residents on a daily basis as a councillor in Oswaldtwistle.

Vote Steven Smithson 💙
Thursday 7th May 🗳️

I was deselected by the Conservatives back in October due to the Conservative Councillor for St Andrews Ward and now Immanuel Conservative Candidate Cllr Steven Smithson being too afraid to fight his own seat and loose to Reform, so abandoned his residents for what he thinks is a "safe Tory seat"

My Immanuel Ward residents deserve better! And deserve someone who isn't going to abandon them at the slightest chance they may loose an election.

That's why I am standing as an Independent Candidate! Because residents deserve someone who will fight for them even when the odds aren't in my favour, they deserve a candidate who will never stop working hard and deserve a candidate who puts them before party politics.

I’m not a career politician; I’m simply a local resident who’s fed up with how 
this country is being run, both locally and nationally. As a father, I am deeply 
concerned for the safety of our children, especially with migrants crossing the 
channel and entering our country illegally. Reform UK is the only way forward 
to keep our community safe. The Conservatives and Labour will promise 
anything to win your vote, only to neglect those same issues once elected. As 
your Reform UK candidate for Accrington North, I will hold the opposition 
parties accountable and work tirelessly to address the pressing issues facing 
our community.

I'm your Labour and Cooperative candidate and current sitting councillor for Netherton Ward.

I've lived in Great Harwood and Rishton all of my life and currently live right in the heart of Netherton. I've worked in local education for 26 years and im committed to honesty and equity.

Noordad Aziz and I work hard together as a team in supporting our neighbours and getting things done.

The Great Harwood Team has 5 members, Me, Noordad, Scott, Heather and Clare, we work together well and all have experience in different areas.

Im a strong advocate for community and am a grassroots councillor, meaning that realistically I see myself as more of a community worker than a councillor. 
Im also a huge advocate for equal rights and fairness and believe that nobody should ever be left behind, especially because of their race, gender, disabilities or other issues.

I have a proven track record and always try my best to help - i also never ever promise anything! Only that I will work hard. Find me on Facebook ♥️

I'm **Zoe Thomas**, your **Green Party** candidate for Overton ward.

I've lived in Great Harwood for two and a half years and I walk these streets every day with my dog Nelson. I care deeply about this community and want to see it thrive.
If elected I will:

- Fight for cleaner streets and tackle fly tipping in our back alleys
- Protect local green spaces from unnecessary development
- Push for genuine community consultation so residents have a real say in decisions that affect them
- Support local businesses and invest in facilities for young people
- Work hard for everyone in Overton ward

The Green Party believes local governance should be done with residents, not to them. I'm listening and I'm ready to work hard for you.
**Vote Green on May 7th**

Hey, I'm Lex, and I’m standing for Pendle and Clitheroe because I believe that our country has been let down by the two main parties, and the Liberal Democrats demonstrated in 2010 that they can’t be trusted either.

The Green Party is the only viable party to keep the next government in check, and I will put the constituency and its people above the political shenanigans we’ve witnessed over the years.

It's embarrassing that only 25% of the Levelling Up fund has been spent in the North, demonstrating once again that we're either forgotten about, or the North is just not cared about. A vote for the Green Party would be a vote for investment in the North of the UK, where we'd aim to tackle all of the problems the constituency currently faces:

- The Cost of Living for many on low wages and high levels of poverty especially of children.

- Shortage of affordable houses to rent or buy especially for young people and coupled trying to set up independent living.

- Fear of being made homeless by no fault evictions or foreclosure of mortgages.

- Failure to access NHS care when they need help now with a long waiting list to be treated.

- Lack of Social Care homes with enough staff, which is creating bed blocking in Hospital.

- Poor connected rail and bus transport, accessible to people on low incomes and those young people in isolated rural areas, who cannot afford their own means of transport.

The Green Party recognises these problems and it's why our main policies are around tackling the Climate Emergency by investing in green jobs and public services which will reduce the cost of living and increase the incomes of citizens.

This investment in people, services and the environment will be paid for by raising the money needed primarily from a wealth tax on rich people and windfall taxes from polluting private companies.

Hi, I'm Lee! 
I'm putting myself forward to be your next councillor! I firmly believe that our local communities deserve better outcomes and needs someone to put your voice at the heart of things.

Having worked in the NHS for the last 10 years, leading on community engagement and patient experiences, I have fixed many a damaged system, - now, I want to do that for local people! Our political system is broken. There's way too much hate, a lot of anger and frustration and not enough visual representation at local level!

People in Rishton are working hard, but too often get left behind - I hear that! I want to be sure progressive change takes place, right from the very start!

It's time for a new local voice, it's time to back Lee Mac!

Promoted by Lee Macneall 40 church street bb67nf on behalf of Lee Macneall Green Party Hyndburn & Green Party England & Wales.

Standing up for you, your family and our community.
I’m proud to be the Conservative candidate for St Oswalds Ward in the LOCAL May 2026
elections. Having lived here for 25 years, this community is my home — and I care deeply
about the people and places that make it so special.
I am actively involved in local life — volunteering at church and supporting community
fundraising projects, but most people will know me best for fighting to protect our precious
green spaces. Since 2021 I have been a lead campaigner against both cemetery applications,
along with other major threats to our green spaces including the BESS application and major
housing developments.
With a background in business, a mother, former teacher, and someone who now cares for
elderly family members, I understand the pressures families face and the importance of
compassion, communication and practical problemsolving.
My commitment to West End, Stanhill, Knuzden, Belthorn and Oswaldtwistle is simple: I will
work hard to protect our area, ensure it remains a priority for council spending, and stand
up for our community through the upcoming Unitary Council changes.
If elected, I will serve with honesty, transparency and respect. My priorities include:
 Protecting and enhancing our green spaces
 Working with police to tackle rising car crime
 Supporting local businesses and community activities
 Keeping our area clean, safe and wellmaintained
 Championing clubs, amenities and opportunities for young people
 Being a visible, approachable councillor who listens
On May 7th, it’s important to vote for someone who truly knows this area — someone
who lives here, understands our challenges, and cares about our future. I will always work
to protect and improve the quality of life for everyone across our ward.
Standing up for you, your family and our community.
Vinette Davitt
Ossyvinette@gmail.com
